@@ -526,3 +526,4 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-zmc3","title":"Task: ntm Template Library for Brenner Prompts","description":"# Goal Create .ntm/templates/ with Brenner-specific prompt templates for operator use. # Why 5 Templates (Restored) They're just text files. Role-specific templates are useful for targeted requests without cluttering the general templates. # The Templates ## 1. brenner_join_session.md For agents just starting - orient them to the session. ```markdown # Session: {{session}} You are joining a Brenner Loop research session. ## Setup 1. Check Agent Mail inbox for thread: {{session}} 2. Read the KICKOFF message to understand the research question 3. Note your assigned role (if role-specific session) ## Your Role Check the thread for role assignment. Common roles: - **Hypothesis Generator**: Propose hypotheses including third alternative - **Test Designer**: Design discriminative tests with potency checks - **Adversarial Critic**: Critique the framing, find real third alternatives ## Response Format Reply with structured DELTA blocks per specs/delta_output_format_v0.1.md ## Ground Rules - Cite transcript anchors (§n) or label claims as [inference] - Include rationale for each delta - Check Agent Mail after posting for feedback ``` ## 2. brenner_request_delta.md General request for structured contribution. ```markdown # DELTA Request: {{session}} Please contribute to thread {{session}} with structured DELTA blocks. ## Requirements 1. Use fenced ```delta code blocks 2. Follow specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md 3. Ground claims in transcript anchors (§n) or label [inference] 4. Include rationale for each delta ## What to Contribute Based on your role and the current artifact state, add: - Hypotheses (including third alternative) - Predictions for each hypothesis - Discriminative tests with potency checks - Assumptions with load/test analysis - Critiques of the framing ## Check Before Submitting - [ ] Each delta has operation, section, payload, rationale - [ ] Claims are grounded or labeled [inference] - [ ] Response addresses gaps in current artifact ``` ## 3. brenner_discriminative_tests.md Role-specific: Test Designer focus. ```markdown # Discriminative Test Request: {{session}} Design discriminative tests for thread {{session}}. ## Test Quality Criteria 1. **Likelihood ratio**: Aim for 10x+ separation between hypotheses 2. **Potency checks**: Include chastity (if test passes, is it meaningful?) and impotence (if test fails, is it informative?) 3. **Expected outcomes**: Specify what each hypothesis predicts 4. **Feasibility**: Note cost, time, and resource requirements ## Output Format ```delta { "operation": "ADD", "section": "discriminative_tests", "payload": { "name": "Test name", "procedure": "Step-by-step procedure", "discriminates": "H1 vs H2", "expected_outcomes": { "H1": "What H1 predicts", "H2": "What H2 predicts" }, "potency_check": "How we know the test is meaningful", "feasibility": "Cost/time estimate" }, "rationale": "Why this test discriminates" } ``` ## Reference - §50: Brenner on discriminative tests - §103: Third alternative thinking - specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md ``` ## 4. brenner_adversarial_critique.md Role-specific: Adversarial Critic focus. ```markdown # Adversarial Critique Request: {{session}} Provide adversarial critique for thread {{session}}. ## What to Attack 1. **Framing assumptions**: What if the question itself is wrong? 2. **Hidden third alternatives**: What are we missing? 3. **Metric confounds**: Are we measuring the right thing? 4. **Exception quarantine**: Are anomalies being swept under Occam's broom? ## Output Format ```delta { "operation": "ADD", "section": "adversarial_critique", "payload": { "name": "Critique name", "attack": "What's wrong with the framing", "evidence": "Why this attack has teeth", "current_status": "Active/needs investigation", "real_third_alternative": true // If this is the REAL alternative we're missing }, "rationale": "Why this critique matters" } ``` ## The Real Third Alternative At least one critique should be marked `real_third_alternative: true`. This isn't just "both hypotheses could be partly right" - it's a genuinely different explanation that reframes the problem. ## Reference - §103-105: Brenner on third alternatives - §160: Exception quarantine ``` ## 5. brenner_round_complete.md Signal end of contribution round. ```markdown # Round Complete: {{session}} This round of {{session}} is complete. Compilation pending. ## What Happens Next 1. Operator runs `./brenner.ts session compile` 2. Artifact is updated with all DELTAs 3. COMPILED message posted to thread 4. Critique round may follow ## What to Do Now - Review any pending ack_required messages - Acknowledge with `mail ack` - Wait for COMPILED artifact before sending more DELTAs ## If You Have Late Contributions Hold them. They'll be included in the next round after compilation. ``` # Implementation ## Directory Structure ``` .ntm/ templates/ brenner_join_session.md brenner_request_delta.md brenner_discriminative_tests.md brenner_adversarial_critique.md brenner_round_complete.md ``` ## Usage ```bash # General requests ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_join_session --all ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_request_delta --all # Role-specific requests ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_discriminative_tests --pane 2 ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_adversarial_critique --pane 3 # End of round ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_round_complete --all ``` ## Variable Substitution - `{{session}}` - ntm substitutes with session name - Future: `{{role}}`, `{{target_repo}}`, `{{artifact_url}}` # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] .ntm/templates/ directory created - [ ] All 5 templates implemented - [ ] Templates reference correct spec sections - [ ] {{session}} substitutes correctly - [ ] Usage documented in cockpit runbook # Relationship to Other Work - Complements: One-command cockpit runner (other agent) - References: specs/delta_output_format_v0.1.md, specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:46.820630462-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:58.87425679-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:10:30.192871341-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: .ntm/config.toml and .ntm/templates/* exist with Brenner templates; issue is redundant.","labels":["infrastructure","ntm","templates"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-zos","title":"TanStack Table: Upgrade CrosswalkTable with Sorting","description":"# Goal Upgrade the CrosswalkTable component to use TanStack Table for proper sorting, filtering, and accessibility. ## Background CrosswalkTable shows a comparison matrix: - Rows: 5 concepts (Problem Selection, Hypothesis Generation, etc.) - Columns: 3 models (Opus, GPT, Gemini) - Cells: Each model's interpretation of the concept Current implementation is a simple map with hover states: ```typescript const CROSSWALK_DATA: CrosswalkRow[] = [ { concept: '...', opus: '...', gpt: '...', gemini: '...' }, // ... 5 rows ]; {CROSSWALK_DATA.map((row, i) => ( setHoveredRow(i)}> {row.concept} {row.opus} {row.gpt} {row.gemini} ))} ``` ## Why TanStack Table? Even for a small table, Table provides: 1. **Sorting**: Click column headers to sort 2. **Column visibility**: Toggle which models to show 3. **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation 4. **Future-proof**: Easy to add filtering, pagination later ## Implementation ### 1. Define columns ```typescript const columns = useMemo(() => [ columnHelper.accessor('concept', { header: 'Concept', cell: info => {info.getValue()}, }), columnHelper.accessor('opus', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), columnHelper.accessor('gpt', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), columnHelper.accessor('gemini', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), ], []); ``` ### 2. Create table instance ```typescript const table = useReactTable({ data: CROSSWALK_DATA, columns, getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(), getSortedRowModel: getSortedRowModel(), state: { sorting }, onSortingChange: setSorting, }); ``` ### 3. Render with Table primitives ```typescript {table.getHeaderGroups().map(headerGroup => ( {headerGroup.headers.map(header => ( ))} ))} {table.getRowModel().rows.map(row => ( {row.getVisibleCells().map(cell => ( ))} ))}
{flexRender(header.column.columnDef.header, header.getContext())} {{ asc: ' ↑', desc: ' ↓', }[header.column.getIsSorted() as string] ?? null}
{flexRender(cell.column.columnDef.cell, cell.getContext())}
``` ## Enhancements Over Current ### Sorting Click any column header to sort: - Alphabetical by concept - Alphabetical by model interpretation ### Column Visibility (Optional) Add toggles to show/hide specific models. Useful for focused comparison. ### Cell Click to Navigate Make cells clickable to navigate to that model's full distillation. ### Keyboard Navigation - Arrow keys to move between cells - Enter to activate cell link - Tab for focus management ## Considerations ### Styling Keep the existing model-specific color coding. Table primitives are unstyled - apply existing Tailwind classes. ### Mobile On mobile, table may need horizontal scroll. Consider: Card view on mobile, table on desktop. ### Row Hover Maintain existing row hover highlight. Table provides row.getIsSelected() etc. for states. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Table uses TanStack Table - [ ] Column sorting works - [ ] Keyboard navigation works - [ ] Existing styling preserved - [ ] Model badges in headers - [ ] Cell links to distillations work ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CrosswalkTable.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:45.917373474-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:55:45.850919825-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:55:45.850919825-05:00","close_reason":"Upgraded CrosswalkTable to use TanStack Table with column sorting, keyboard navigation (Enter/Space), ARIA attributes (role=grid, aria-sort), and sort indicators. Build passes. Preserved existing styling and tooltips.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-zos","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:19:20.347689225-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-zsq","title":"Quote bank: normalize operator mapping + tags","description":"Normalize quote bank into operator-keyed mapping (⊘/𝓛/⧉/≡/✂/⟂/↑/⇓/ΔE/∿/⊙) with a canonical tag taxonomy; keep file sprawl minimal and ensure every operator has ≥3 grounded quotes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:00:57.677703996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.852618607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.852618607-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.2.3 (Quote bank normalization)"} +{"id":"brenner_bot-zzc7","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: delta-parser.ts parsing logic","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:50:49.598280249-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:50:49.598280249-05:00"}
